topics: Fundamental Concepts: CIA Triad (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability, Authentication, Authorization, and Accountability)
Common Threats and Vulnerabilities: Malware (Viruses, Worms, Trojans), Phishing, Social Engineering, Denial of Service (DoS) Attacks
Security Policies and Risk Management: Developing and Implementing Security Policies, Risk Assessment and Mitigation
Network Security Protocols: IPsec, SSL/TLS, VPNs
Firewalls and Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS): Types of Firewalls
Wireless Network Security: Wireless Encryption Protocols (WEP, WPA2)
Introduction to Software Security: Importance of Secure Software
Common Software Vulnerabilities: Buffer Overflows, SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
Secure Coding Practices: Input Validation and Sanitization
Security in Software Development: Secure Software Development Frameworks
